description: Two photos of tall fescue under acute drought stress in Kansas. Tests that the AI prioritizes rehydration over other recommendations and uses correct drought-stress guidance.

photos.caption: Wide view of tall fescue lawn with widespread grayish-blue wilting and folding leaf blades — classic drought stress color change before browning.
Close-up of tall fescue blades showing rolled or folded leaf blades and dull gray-green color indicating water deficit stress.

profile.weatherSummary: 94 F, sunny, no rain in 3 weeks
profile.notes: Lawn has turned grayish and blades are folding. Footprints stay visible in the grass. No irrigation in the past 2 weeks due to travel.
profile.currentRoutine: Normally mow at 3.5 inches weekly and water twice a week, but missed watering for 2 weeks while traveling.

groundTruth.photoNotes: Two photos of tall fescue in acute drought stress. Wide shot shows widespread grayish-blue wilting canopy with folded blades. Close-up confirms rolled leaf blades and dull color — classic pre-dormancy wilt response to water deficit. No signs of disease or pest damage.
